name: cwd-first-fallback-default-repo-pattern
tags: cwd, cross-repo, node design, fan-out, task context, session_graph, resolve, fallback
body:
UPS pipeline nodes that resolve a repo-scoped index/resource must try `state["cwd"]`'s repo first, falling back to a default repo (claude-hooks) only if nothing exists there — never hardcode a single repo. `load_task_code.py` established this pattern; `load_related_commits.py` was fixed to match it (bug task:4b7ccfe8).

Why: a task can be activated from any repo, not just claude-hooks-dev/-test/-main. A node that skips the cwd check doesn't fail loudly on a cross-repo task — it silently returns data from the wrong repo (e.g. claude-hooks commits injected as "related" for an ecommerce-blast-radius task), which is worse than returning nothing. Design intent: a task should behave the same way regardless of which repo it lives in.

How to apply: when adding a new UPS node that reads a repo-local file/index (TurboVec .tvim, git log, etc.), resolve the path as `Path(state["cwd"]) / <resource>` first; only fall back to a fixed default path if the cwd-scoped resource doesn't exist. Apply this check whenever reviewing or adding fan-out nodes in langchain_learning/nodes/.
